summary.intent: Merge main into a feature branch (feat/issue-275-openai-responses), resolve merge conflicts, address all PR review comments from PR #361, then commit and push.
summary.outcome: All 4 merge conflicts resolved, PR review fixes applied (ProviderRaw events, double-Done guard via finalize_responses_stream, URL normalization), 225 tests passing, formatting fixed, and changes pushed to the correct fork remote. PR #361 shows clean/mergeable.

repo: The project uses a fork-based contribution model: PR #361 is from fork thomas7725353/moltis, not moltis-org/moltis. The fork remote must be added separately to push to the PR branch.

main refactored Responses API code: split_responses_instructions_and_input and finalize_responses_stream were moved to openai_compat.rs and made public, while parse_responses_completion was added there too.

The repo uses cargo fmt nightly-2025-11-30 for formatting checks and enforces import ordering — all use items in a super:: block must be sorted alphabetically on a single conceptual line with no mid-block line breaks.
CSS must be built via a separate Tailwind step; style.css is not committed and a missing build artifact causes cargo check to fail.
The project uses a just-based local CI with parallel checks: fmt, biome, i18n, zizmor. Commit status publishing to forks requires repo:status PAT scope which may not be available.
Version scheme in Cargo.toml moved to date-based (0.1.0 on main vs prior scheme on branch).

code.finding: Extra blank line before #[cfg(test)] mod tests caused fmt check failure — must be exactly one blank line.
Extra blank line between closing brace of a function and the next #[allow(clippy::collapsible_if)] attribute caused fmt check failure.
Import list from super::openai_compat must be formatted with rustfmt's alphabetical, line-length-aware grouping — manual line breaks that don't match rustfmt's output will fail the format check.

workflow: When using superset worktrees, the local branch name (e.g., thomas7725353/feat/issue-275-openai-responses) differs from the PR's remote branch name (feat/issue-275-openai-responses) — always verify PR head ref before pushing.
GitHub PR conflict status can lag behind the actual remote state; checking mergeable via gh api is more reliable than the UI.
force-with-lease fails if the local ref tracking is stale after adding a new remote — fetch first, then force-push.
Compile early after resolving conflicts; CSS build artifacts are a prerequisite for clean cargo check output.

friction: Pushed to the wrong remote (moltis-org/moltis) instead of the fork (thomas7725353/moltis), requiring discovery of the fork model mid-session.
Branch tracking was not set up in the worktree, so the initial push went to a non-PR branch and the PR did not update.
force-with-lease failed on first attempt because the local tracking ref for the fork was stale; required fetch before force-push.
GitHub PR UI showed stale conflict state after push, requiring API verification to confirm the update took effect.
CSS build artifact (style.css) was missing, causing an unrelated cargo check error that needed a separate npm install + build step.
Commit status publishing to the fork failed with HTTP 404 due to insufficient PAT scope (repo:status), producing noisy hook output.
open_items: The release.yml change was reverted as unnecessary — the original motivation for that change is unresolved/unknown and may need follow-up.
Commit status publishing hooks cannot write to the fork repo due to PAT permission limitations; this is a known limitation left unresolved.
